metadata.title: Map Your Build Decisions to What Students Are Reading with Claude Code

beats.narration: You just built a grading tool with hooks and subagents. Here is the exact chapter in the students book that corresponds to every decision you made.
The teacher who has lived through the build knows something that makes the students book chapter real. The mapping tool turns that experience into pedagogical content the teacher can share on Monday.

beats.narration_text: The same scaffolding principle that keeps a student from outsourcing their thinking protects a musician from outsourcing their voice. When Claude drafts, you judge. When Claude suggests a pitch angle, you decide if it's true. The tool that flags 'this is AI doing your creative work for you' is the tool that keeps your music yours.

artifactLines: The same tools the platforms use — pointed back at you.
Roll your own. Own the deck.

beats.narration: Ask Claude to map five build decisions to chapters in Claude Code for Students — each with a key concept and a ten-minute classroom activity.

props.command:
claude "Map these build decisions to chapters
  in Claude Code for Students:
  (1) wrote CLAUDE.md before first session
  (2) used specification prompt, 5 elements
  (3) ran plausibility probe on sort function
  (4) added PreToolUse hook, blocked grade output
  (5) deployed pattern-analyzer subagent
  For each: chapter, key concept, 10-min activity."

beats.narration: Five decisions mapped to five chapters: CLAUDE.md to ch.3 with a write-CLAUDE.md activity, specification prompt to ch.8 with a fill-spec-template activity, plausibility probe to ch.5 with a run-tie-case activity, hook to ch.8 with a read-the-hook activity, subagent to ch.10 with a measure-context activity.

props.code:
# Build-to-Curriculum Mapping

| Decision                | Chapter | Key Concept      | Activity              |
|------------------------|---------|------------------|-----------------------|
| Wrote CLAUDE.md first  | ch.3    | Context = code   | Write CLAUDE.md for a toy project |
| 5-element spec prompt  | ch.8    | Spec vs. vague   | Fill spec template for GPA sort |
| Plausibility probe     | ch.5    | PA capacity      | Run tie-case probe live |
| PreToolUse hook        | ch.8    | Hooks = enforce  | Read block-grades.sh, predict what it blocks |
| Pattern-analyzer agent | ch.10   | Subagent isolation | Measure context before/after subagent |

beats.narration: Five decisions, five chapter mappings. Each row has a concrete ten-minute classroom activity that uses the same concept the teacher implemented. No read the book activities — all are active: write, fill, run, read, measure. The teacher's build experience becomes Monday's lesson.
Map Act Three build decisions — three-file system and deployment verification — and verify they map to the correct chapters.

props.command:
claude "Run mapping on Act Three build decisions:
  three-file system, deployment verification.
  Verify three-file maps to ch.7 (SDD)
  and deployment maps to ch.13 (verification)."

beats.narration: Act Three mapping: three-file system maps to ch.7 — the SDD is the decision document before the build; activity: write a DESIGN.md for a color palette before generating any UI. Deployment verification maps to ch.13 — three-pass verification protocol; activity: run Pass 3 on a running build and find one SDD gap. Both map correctly.

The mapping tool is a curriculum alignment tool. The teacher builds the tool, maps the decisions to chapters, and walks into class with five activities drawn directly from experience. The students read the theory; the teacher has the practice; the mapping closes the gap.
That completes the Claude Code for Teachers series. Explore the full playlist for more.
Like and subscribe for more Claude Code for Teachers.
